data.description:
## Context
Perform a read-only principal-engineer audit of rebar against modern software architecture and open-source community standards. Examine README.md, pyproject.toml, src/rebar/, tests/, docs/architecture.md, docs/adr/, CI workflows, packaging metadata, security posture, extension contracts, and contributor governance.

## Plan
- Map subsystem boundaries, dependency direction, data ownership, public APIs, CLI/MCP adapters, and the git-backed event store.
- Inspect tests, CI, release/packaging, security, documentation, accessibility of contribution, and project governance.
- Ground material findings in exact repository evidence and distinguish strengths, risks, and tradeoffs.
- Produce a prioritized remediation roadmap without modifying production code.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The audit explains the architecture and identifies evidence-backed strengths.
- [ ] Material risks are prioritized by severity and include concrete code or documentation citations.
- [ ] The assessment covers OSS baseline artifacts, packaging, CI, security, governance, contributor experience, testing, and operability.
- [ ] Recommendations distinguish near-term fixes from longer-term architectural investments.
- [ ] Audit observations and final conclusions are recorded on this ticket.
data.title: Principal architecture and OSS standards audit
